Registry / Terms / initial verification

initial verification

—Not in VIM/VIML G 18 #00194 · 9 instances

Suggested actions

  1. HIGH No authoritative definition in VIM or VIML.
  2. MEDIUM 7 distinct definitions — harmonise.

Publication instances

Ed.YearPublicationClauseDefinitionConsistency
20102004OIML D 9:2004
#00194
2.12
verification of a measuring instrument which has not been verified previously [VIML, 15]
pending
20102004OIML R 111-1:2004
#01469
2.18.1
(A series of tests and visual examinations carried out before the equipment/weight is put into service to determine whether a weight or weight set has been manufactured to replicate a given type and conforms to that type and to regulations, and that its metrological characteristics lie within the limits required for initial verification of copies of that type. If the weights or weight set pass all the tests and examinations, it is given legal character byits acceptance as evidenced by stamping and/or the issuing of acertificate of verification dapted from OIML D 20 Initial and subsequent verification of measuring instruments and processes (1988))
pending
20101986OIML D 15:1986
#00262
2.5
the verification of a new measuring instrument which has not been verified previously [VML 2.4.2].
pending

#03139
3.1.5
verification of a measuring instrument which has not been verified previously (OIML V 1, 2.12)
pending

#03138
3.1.6
verification of a measuring instrument which has not been verified previously [from VIML, 2.12 [3]]
pending

verification of a measuring instrument which has not been verified previously [VIML 2.15]
pending

verification of a measuring instrument which has not been verified previously [VIML, 15]
pending
202X2004OIML R 111-1:2004
#01469
2.18.1
series of tests and visual examinations carried out before the equipment/weight is put into service to determine whether a weight or weight set has been manufactured to replicate a given type and conforms to that type and to regulations, and that its metrological characteristics lie within the limits required for initial verification of copies of that type. If the weights or weight set pass all the tests and examinations, it is given legal character by its acceptance as evidenced by stamping and/or the issuing of a certificate of verification (Adapted from OIML D 20 Initial and subsequent verification of measuring instruments and processes (1988))
pending
202X1986OIML D 15:1986
#00262
2.5
the verification of a new measuring instrument which has not been verified previously [VML 2.4.2].
